stories.headline: SEC and CFTC sign historic MOU, ending years of jurisdictional turf war
stories.tldr: The SEC and CFTC announced their first formal memorandum of understanding to coordinate crypto oversight, codifying a joint interpretation issued earlier this spring.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A unified federal posture takes the regulator-arbitrage card off the table for issuers and squeezes Congress to ratify market-structure law before agencies improvise another framework.
the_details: Press release 2026-26 confirms shared rulemaking, examinations and enforcement coordination across the two agencies.
Builds on the March SEC interpretation clarifying which crypto assets fall under federal securities laws.
Follows last week's letter from 100+ firms pushing the Senate Banking Committee to mark up the Clarity Act.

stories.headline: Volo Protocol drained for $3.5M as Aave bleeds $9B in post-Kelp exodus
stories.tldr: A second DeFi protocol fell days after the $292M Kelp DAO drain, while Aave logged roughly $9B in withdrawals as restaking risk spreads through lending markets.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: April's $606M loss tally is now reshaping DeFi plumbing in real time — LSTs and rsETH-style collateral are repricing, and lenders are forced to rewrite oracle and bridge assumptions.
the_details: Volo Protocol confirmed a $3.5M exploit on April 22, three days after the Kelp DAO breach.
Bloomberg reported roughly $9B has exited Aave since April 19 amid cross-chain restaking contagion fears.
April 2026 is now the worst month for crypto theft since February 2025's $1.4B Bybit hack.

stories.headline: Bitcoin-dollar correlation hits -0.90, the deepest inverse since 2022
stories.tldr: BTC's 30-day correlation with the U.S. Dollar Index has collapsed to -0.90, the most negative reading in nearly four years as DXY weakens into the April Fed meeting.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A clean inverse to the dollar reframes BTC as a macro hedge rather than a risk-on tech proxy, and raises the bar for whether ETF flows can sustain the rally if the dollar bounces.
the_details: BTC is holding above $77,000, up more than 13% on the month and tracking its best April since 2025.
The next test is the April FOMC, with $79K either flipping to support or fading back to a $75K-$77K range.
USDT supply expanded $5B in April, nearing $150B and underwriting much of the spot bid.

stories.headline: SoFi opens XRP deposits as GraniteShares lists 3x leveraged XRP ETFs on Nasdaq
stories.tldr: SoFi enabled XRP deposits for retail customers and GraniteShares launched 3x long and short daily XRP ETFs on Nasdaq, deepening XRP's institutional rails.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Combined retail onramp and leveraged ETF tooling pulls XRP further from its courtroom past and into the same product stack as BTC and ETH — a template altcoins like SOL will try to copy.
the_details: SoFi now accepts XRP deposits, with withdrawals limited to external wallets at launch.
Seven spot XRP ETFs now hold a combined ~$1B in AUM after the SEC and CFTC's commodity classification.
XRP trades near $1.43 after settling around $1.40 earlier in April.

stories.headline: BIP 361 lays out three-phase migration to quantum-resistant Bitcoin
stories.tldr: Bitcoin Improvement Proposal 361, published this month, outlines a three-phase migration of UTXOs to post-quantum signature schemes ahead of expected hardware threats.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Quantum risk has shifted from think-piece to active engineering — protocols that don't have a credible migration plan now look exposed, while Bitcoin's roadmap converts a tail risk into a coordination problem.
the_details: BIP 361 proposes opt-in then mandatory phases for moving funds to quantum-resistant outputs.
Authors frame it as defensive prep, not a response to any current break in elliptic-curve cryptography.
Discussion is now active across Bitcoin Core, mining pools and large custodians on signaling timelines.
